channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Mexico's New Cyber Plan Faces Its First Real Test
item.description: The Latin American nation's cybersecurity plan — still in the expansion phase — has to survive its own knockout round during the FIFA World Cup....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Mount Royal University confirms breach as hackers claim attack
item.description: Mount Royal University in Calgary says hackers stole and then deleted data from its file storage systems after breaching the university's network. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Lone Attacker Uses AI to Breach AWS Cloud Environment in 72 Hours
item.description: The attacker exploited AI workflows, chained cloud weaknesses, and stolen credentials to extort a large Amazon customer....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Fake Paysafe, Skrill SDKs on NPM and PyPi steal credentials
item.description: Malicious packages on the Node Package Manager (npm) and the Python Package Index (PyPI) delivered stealer malware to developers and users of Paysafe, Skrill, and Neteller payment applications. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers exploit Roundcube flaw to spy on academic researchers
item.description: A China-linked threat cluster has been exploiting vulnerable Roundcube servers at U.S. and Canadian universities to steal credentials and deploy backdoor malware. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI Coding Agents Found Triggering Endpoint Security Rules Built to Catch Attackers
item.description:
Sophos looked at a week of its own endpoint data and found that AI coding agents such as Claude Code, Cursor, and OpenAI Codex are setting off detection rules written to catch human intruders.
The ag...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Entra passkey enrollment vishing targets Microsoft 365 users
item.description: A threat actor has been targeting organizations across multiple sectors with voice-based fake security requests that ask Microsoft 365 users to enroll a new Entra passkey. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Vidar Infostealer Hammers SMBs via Malvertising Campaign
item.description: A financially motivated operation uses lures of cracked or pirated software to deliver a malware two-for-one combo for data theft and cryptomining....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New HalluSquatting Attack Could Trick AI Coding Assistants Into Installing Botnet Malware
item.description:
AI coding assistants have a habit of making things up. Ask one to fetch a popular tool, and it will sometimes hand back a real-sounding name for a project that does not exist.
New research, which its...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ubiquiti Patches Critical UniFi Flaws Across Connect, Talk, Access, Protect, and OS
item.description: Ubiquiti has shipped updates to address multiple critical security flaws impacting UniFi Connect, UniFi Talk, UniFi Access, UniFi Protect, and UniFi OS that could result in privilege escalation and ar...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 3 Ways AI Powers Service Desk Attacks and How to Prevent Them
item.description: Specops Software explains how AI is making service desk impersonation attacks more convincing, personalized, and scalable, along with practical steps organizations can take to strengthen onboarding an...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New Ghost Phishing Wave Is Breaking Traditional Email Security
item.description: A recent EvilTokens campaign targeting businesses across the US and Europe is exposing a new email security blind spot. This “ghost phishing” technique keeps the malicious page hidden until it decrypt...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: SCMBANKER Malware Uses ClickFix Lures to Target Mexican Banking Users
item.description:
A new banking fraudulent operation is targeting customers of Mexican banks, fintech, payment processors, and cryptocurrency exchanges using ClickFix lures.
The activity cluster, tracked by Elastic Se...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Felons, Fraudsters Flog Offensive Cybersecurity Startup
item.description: A cybersecurity startup dangling millions of dollars to acquire zero-day security vulnerabilities in popular software is run by a pair of far-right conspiracy theorists and convicted felons whose most...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: DuckDuckGo browser now blocks YouTube video ads
item.description: DuckDuckGo announced that its browser can now block most video ads on YouTube, including those shown before the video starts playing and during playback. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: GitHub 'Verified' Commits Can Be Rewritten Into New Hashes Without Breaking Signatures
item.description: New research shows that a signed Git commit's hash is not the one-of-a-kind name that much of the software world assumes it to be. Given any signed commit, someone without the signing key can mint a s...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Verification Step Is the New ATO Battleground in 2026
item.description: For years, account takeover (ATO) followed a predictable script. Attackers bought stolen credentials in bulk, ran them through automated tools, and waited for matches. Credential stuffing was cheap, s...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Telco giant KDDI says data breach affects over 12 million people
item.description: Japanese telecommunications giant KDDI says that millions of people had their email addresses and passwords exposed after attackers breached an email platform used by five internet service providers (...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: GitHub Copilot Refuses Harmful Requests in Chat, Then Writes Them in Code
item.description: An AI coding assistant that refuses to answer a dangerous request in its chat box can answer it anyway if the same request is broken into small, ordinary-looking steps inside a code editor. That is th...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA orders feds to prioritize patching Langflow auth bypass flaw
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) gave federal agencies until Friday to patch an actively exploited vulnerability in the Langflow visual framework for building AI agents...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: China-Linked UAT-7810 Expands ORB Network With New LONGLEASH Malware
item.description:
A Chinese threat actor tracked as UAT-7810 is actively refining its bespoke malware to expand its Operational Relay Box (ORB) network by breaking into internet-facing networking devices.
According to...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ubiquiti warns of new max severity UniFi OS vulnerability
item.description: Ubiquiti has released security updates to patch seven critical vulnerabilities in UniFi OS, including a maximum-severity flaw that can be exploited in command injection attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: State IDs for AI Agents: Will Estonia Set a Precedent?
item.description: The world's digital testing ground plans to help people use AI agents for government purposes....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: CISA orders feds to patch max severity ColdFusion flaw by Friday
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has ordered government agencies to patch an actively exploited maximum-severity flaw in the Adobe ColdFusion commercial web app develop...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 15-Year-Old GhostLock Flaw Enables Root and Container Escape on Most Linux Distros
item.description:
Researchers at Nebula Security have disclosed GhostLock (CVE-2026-43499), a 15-year-old Linux kernel flaw that lets any logged-in user take full root control of a machine that has not been patched.
T...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA Adds 4 Actively Exploited Adobe, Joomla, and Langflow Flaws to KEV
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added four security flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation.
The...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Accenture confirms breach after hacker offers stolen data for sale
item.description: IT services giant Accenture has confirmed it suffered a security breach after a threat actor claimed to have stolen 35 GB of source code and other data from the company. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Big Brand Jobs Scam Targets Marketing Pros' Google Accounts
item.description: The phishing campaign uses several tactics, including nested redirects, to evade detection and steal credentials from unsuspecting targets....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Dialogflow CX 'Rogue Agent' Flaw Enabled AI Chatbot Data Theft
item.description: Varonis reported the flaw to Google in late 2025 and it has been addressed, but it reminds defenders to take a fresh look at their AI Infrastructure security....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Chinese hackers develop LONGLEASH malware to expand ORB network
item.description: Chinese hackers tracked as 'UAT-7810' are actively evolving their malware to expand their Operational Relay Box (ORB) network by compromising internet-facing networking devices, primarily unpatched Ru...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
